action_set_definitions.name: search_inventory
action_set_definitions.description: Searches the content inventory for items matching specific keywords or criteria.

query.description: Keywords related to the research topic (e.g., 'Victorian network correspondence').

action_set_definitions.name: get_paper_details
action_set_definitions.description: Retrieves comprehensive metadata for a specific content item, including status and classification.

item_id.description: The unique identifier of the content item.

action_set_definitions.name: analyze_cross_domain_relationship
action_set_definitions.description: Calculates semantic similarity, citation overlap, and direct link status between two items from different domains.

source_item_id.description: The ID of the foundational historical paper.

target_item_id.description: The ID of the technological bridge paper.

action_set_definitions.name: submit_recommendation_pair
action_set_definitions.description: Finalizes the curation by recommending a specific pair of items to the user.

history_item_id.description: The ID of the Victorian network study.

tech_item_id.description: The ID of the GNN sparse data paper.

state_description.user_profile: Mapping of content categories to interest scores (0.0 to 1.0) and latent preference vectors.
state_description.session_history: A chronological list of item IDs previously presented to the user in the current session.
state_description.engagement_metrics: Cumulative session stats including total_dwell_time, total_clicks, and bounce_count.
state_description.content_inventory: A pool of available content items currently available for recommendation, including metadata for each.
state_description.user_satisfaction: A hidden numerical value representing the user's current level of satisfaction with the curation.

metadata.title: Victorian Correspondence Networks: A Structural Analysis
A Comprehensive Survey of 19th Century Letter Exchange
Graph Neural Networks for Sparse Interaction Datasets
The Evolution of Global Postal Logistics
Attention Mechanisms in Large Language Models
